content.text: detects it, extracts the code, and runs it in a subprocess or Docker container. The stdout and stderr are captured and posted back into the conversation as a reply so the assistant can read the output and react (fix errors, continue with the results, or declare success).

attrs.alt: AutoGen code execution flow: AssistantAgent writes a Python block; UserProxyAgent detects it and routes to Docker or local subprocess; stdout/stderr posted back into the conversation

content.text: Code execution is configured via the
Local directory where scripts are written and run. Created if it does not exist.
True uses the default python:3-slim image; a string like 'python:3.11' pins a specific image; False runs in the local process.
Seconds before the subprocess is killed (default: 60).
How many recent messages to scan for code blocks. 'auto' means scan all.

from autogen import UserProxyAgent

# Local execution (no Docker required)
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "user_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config={
        "work_dir": "agent_workspace",  # scripts land here
        "use_docker": False,            # run in local Python process
        "timeout": 30,                  # kill after 30 s
        "last_n_messages": 3,           # scan last 3 messages for code
    },
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)

(or a specific image tag) runs every code block inside an isolated container. This is the safest option for untrusted or unpredictable code — the container has no access to your host filesystem beyond a mounted
, and is discarded after each run.
the Docker daemon must be running on the host. AutoGen pulls the image automatically on first use. On CI runners, add the docker service to your pipeline configuration.

runs code in a subprocess on the host. This is simpler to set up and faster (no container spin-up) but runs code with your current user's permissions. Use it for trusted, deterministic tasks — not for arbitrary agent-generated code in production.

from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent

llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "api_key": "sk-..."}]}

assistant = AssistantAgent("assistant", llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You write Python code to solve mathematical tasks.")
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "user_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    max_consecutive_auto_reply=5,
    code_execution_config={
        "work_dir": "math_ws",
        "use_docker": False,
    },
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)

user_proxy.initiate_chat(
    assistant,
    message="Write and run Python code that computes the first 10 Fibonacci numbers.",
)

to disable code execution entirely. Use this when an agent should only reason and reply, never run code. This is common for inner group-chat managers, summariser agents, and agents in the outer layer of a nested-chat hierarchy.

from autogen import UserProxyAgent

# No code execution -- pure conversation relay
outer_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "outer_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config=False,   # disables execution
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)
When the assistant writes code and the proxy executes it, both agents take turns automatically. The
caps how many times the proxy can reply consecutively without human input. This prevents runaway execution loops when the assistant keeps writing buggy code and the proxy keeps running it.

user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "user_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    max_consecutive_auto_reply=5,   # after 5 auto-replies, stop
    code_execution_config={
        "work_dir": "ws",
        "use_docker": False,
        "timeout": 30,
    },
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)
After the conversation ends, execution results are in the message history. Filter the transcript to extract output from specific runs — execution result messages include

from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent

llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "api_key": "sk-..."}]}
assistant = AssistantAgent("assistant", llm_config=llm_config)
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "user_proxy", human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config={"work_dir": "out_ws", "use_docker": False},
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""))

result = user_proxy.initiate_chat(
    assistant,
    message="Write and run Python code that prints the sum of numbers 1 to 100.",
    max_turns=4,
)

# Chat history is result.chat_history (list of dicts)
for msg in result.chat_history:
    content = msg.get("content") or ""
    if "exitcode" in content:   # execution result messages contain exitcode
        print(f"Execution output:\n{content}")
Missing work_dir permissions: if the work_dir is under a path your user cannot write to, scripts silently fail. Use a relative path like 'ws' that is created in the current working directory.
Timeout too short: complex computation or pip installs can exceed the default 60 s. Raise timeout in code_execution_config when the task is known to take longer.
Code block language mismatch: AutoGen only runs blocks tagged ```python or ```sh. A block tagged ```python3 or with no language tag is not executed.
use_docker=True without Docker running: if the Docker daemon is not available, AutoGen raises an error at the first code block. Check with 'docker info' before running.
Multiple code blocks in one message: AutoGen executes only the last code block in a message by default. The assistant should write one complete script per message.
Installing Dependencies in the Execution Environment
Agent-generated code often imports third-party packages. In local mode, the packages must already be installed in the active environment. In Docker mode, you can instruct the assistant to include
commands in a shell block before the Python code, or you can build a custom image with the dependencies pre-installed.

# Approach 1: pip install in a preceding ```sh block (Docker or local)
# The assistant writes two blocks:
#   ```sh
#   pip install numpy pandas
#   ```
#   ```python
#   import numpy as np
#   print(np.random.rand(3))
#   ```

# Approach 2: pre-built Docker image with dependencies
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "user_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config={
        "work_dir": "data_ws",
        # Custom image already has numpy, pandas, matplotlib
        "use_docker": "myorg/data-agent:1.0",
        "timeout": 120,
    },
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)
calls in shell blocks run as your user and modify your environment. If you allow arbitrary shell execution, consider using a virtual environment or Docker to isolate package installs.
For production deployments where the assistant may generate untrusted code, you can restrict execution by subclassing
to apply an allowlist or sandbox. Alternatively, limit
to 1 so only the most recent code block is ever executed, or wrap the execution in a policy check before dispatching.

. Files written by one execution are available to the next execution in the same conversation because the working directory persists for the life of the session. The assistant can write a CSV in one turn and read it in another, building up results incrementally.

from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent

llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "api_key": "sk-..."}]}

assistant = AssistantAgent("assistant", llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message=(
        "You are a data analyst. Write Python scripts that produce files in the "
        "working directory. Build on files from previous turns when they exist."
    ))
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "user_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config={"work_dir": "analysis_ws", "use_docker": False},
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)

# Turn 1: generate data
user_proxy.initiate_chat(assistant,
    message="Generate a CSV file named 'sales.csv' with 20 rows of synthetic sales data.",
    max_turns=3)

# Turn 2: analyse data (sales.csv is still present in analysis_ws)
user_proxy.initiate_chat(assistant,
    message="Read 'sales.csv' and compute the total and average sales by region.",
    max_turns=4)

When executed code raises an exception or returns a non-zero exit code, the output (the traceback) is posted back into the conversation. The assistant sees the error and typically rewrites the code to fix it. Each retry increments the auto-reply counter toward

from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent

llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "api_key": "sk-..."}]}

assistant = AssistantAgent("assistant", llm_config=llm_config)
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "user_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    max_consecutive_auto_reply=8,   # allow several fix iterations
    code_execution_config={
        "work_dir": "debug_ws",
        "use_docker": False,
        "timeout": 30,
    },
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)

user_proxy.initiate_chat(
    assistant,
    message=(
        "Write a Python function that reads a JSON file named 'config.json' "
        "and prints the 'version' key. The file does not exist yet, so your "
        "code must handle FileNotFoundError gracefully."
    ),
)
# If the first attempt raises FileNotFoundError, the assistant will
# see the traceback and add a try/except in the next attempt.
To unit-test pipelines that involve code execution, register a mock reply function on the assistant that returns a pre-written code block, and set
so execution is deterministic. Assert on the exit code and the content posted back by the proxy.

from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent

def test_code_runs_and_returns_output():
    assistant = AssistantAgent("assistant", llm_config=False)
    # Mock reply: the assistant always writes this code block
    assistant.register_reply(
        trigger=None,
        reply_func=lambda r, msgs, s, cfg: (
            True,
            "```python\nprint('hello from code execution')\n```\n"
        ),
        position=0,
    )
    proxy = UserProxyAgent(
        "proxy",
        human_input_mode="NEVER",
        max_consecutive_auto_reply=1,
        code_execution_config={"work_dir": "test_ws", "use_docker": False},
        is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
    )
    result = proxy.initiate_chat(assistant, message="run code", max_turns=2)
    outputs = [m.get("content","") for m in result.chat_history]
    assert any("hello from code execution" in o for o in outputs), (
        "expected code output not found in chat history"
    )

test_code_runs_and_returns_output()
print("Test passed.")
In a group chat, only the
executes code. When the coder writes a Python block, the manager routes the next turn to the proxy (either via
selection or an explicit transition). The proxy's execution output lands in the shared message history for all agents to see.

from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, GroupChat, GroupChatManager

llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "api_key": "sk-..."}]}

coder = AssistantAgent("coder", llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You write Python code. Include a complete runnable script.")
reviewer = AssistantAgent("reviewer", llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You review code and execution output for correctness.")
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "user_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config={"work_dir": "gc_ws", "use_docker": False},
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)

gc = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, coder, reviewer],
    messages=[],
    max_round=8,
    # After coder writes code, proxy executes; after execution, reviewer checks
    allowed_or_disallowed_speaker_transitions={
        coder: [user_proxy],
        user_proxy: [reviewer, coder],
        reviewer: [coder, user_proxy],
    },
    speaker_transitions_type="allowed",
)
mgr = GroupChatManager(groupchat=gc, llm_config=llm_config)
user_proxy.initiate_chat(mgr,
    message="Write a Python function that reverses a string and test it.")
The UserProxyAgent extracts ```python blocks from the assistant's messages and runs them in a subprocess or Docker container; stdout/stderr are posted back into the conversation.
code_execution_config accepts work_dir, use_docker (bool or image tag), timeout, and last_n_messages.
use_docker=True runs code in an isolated container (safer for arbitrary code); use_docker=False runs in a local subprocess (simpler setup).
Set code_execution_config=False to disable execution entirely for relay or orchestration agents.
max_consecutive_auto_reply caps automatic reply loops to prevent runaway execution cycles.
Only blocks tagged ```python or ```sh are executed; one complete script per message is the expected pattern.
